title: HTML Fixtures, Fake Transports, Local Servers, Properties, Faults, and Mutation · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: An internal authorized product-quality engineering group. They need rendering only when necessary, isolated Playwright contexts, block stop policy, SSRF and output-injection defense, versioned accessible reports, deterministic local-server faults, properties, and mutation evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Secure Dynamic Crawl, Export, and Verification System with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Compare server HTML, embedded data, public API, progressive enhancement, and browser output and render only when the stakeholder evidence requires it.
Treat CAPTCHA and human-verification challenges as stop and owner-coordination signals rather than puzzles to automate around.
Allowlist schemes and destinations, reject credentials and dangerous address classes, constrain ports, and separate user input from fetch authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Render Necessity Decision
steps.instruction:
project case — A project site-quality team handles case 5186 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine fixed HTML, fake transports, local servers, URL properties, fault injection, and mutation evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare server HTML, embedded data, public API, progressive enhancement, and browser output and render only when the stakeholder evidence requires it.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Mutation Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-mutation-evidence-cm4k99" that uses case 4550 to combine fixed HTML, fake transports, local servers, URL properties, fault injection, and mutation evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-mutation-evidence-cm4k99
# Competency: scraper-mutation-evidence.
# Case 4550: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_mutation_evidence_cm4k99(fixtures: int = 72, changed_cases: int = 36, injected_faults: int = 18, killed_mutants: int = 16, live_public_requests: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_cm4k99 = "cm4k99-99k4mc-4550"
    assert evidence_variant_cm4k99
    evidence_axes_cm4k99 = (
        "authorizationcm4k99",
        "url4550",
        "response99k4mc",
        "treecm4k994550",
        "frontier4550cm4k99",
        "report99k4mc4550",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_cm4k99)) == 6
    if fixtures < 24 or changed_cases < 12:
        return False, fixtures, "test-depth"
    if injected_faults < 8:
        return False, injected_faults, "fault-depth"
    if killed_mutants < 8:
        return False, killed_mutants, "mutation-evidence"
    if live_public_requests > 0:
        return False, live_public_requests, "nondeterministic-live-test"
    assert killed_mutants >= 8 and live_public_requests == 0
    return True, killed_mutants, "layered-crawler-verification"
